paper_title: Untargeted metabolomic analysis of tomato pollen development and heat stress response

methods.description: Statistical procedures applied to metabolomics data and pollen viability data, including ANOVA, post hoc tests, and Principal Component Analysis (PCA). Analyses were performed using GenStat 18th Edition, IBM SPSS statistic software package 20, and GeneMaths XT v.2.12.

steps.description: A univariate ANOVA analysis was performed for each annotated metabolite with a treatment structure: condition factor x development factor and a block structure considering the number of observations within plants.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform univariate ANOVA on log2 transformed metabolite data with specified factors and block structure.
outputs: ANOVA results (p-values, F-statistics) for each metabolite

steps.description: Due to the large number of variables generated by metabolomics analysis, 41 secondary metabolites, the statistically significant p value threshold was adjusted for multiple testing. A p value of 0.01 was used as a threshold.
steps.searchable_summary: Set p-value threshold for significance after multiple testing correction.

steps.description: The ANOVA of metabolites with a statistically significant p value was followed by a Bonferroni's post hoc test to correct for the number of analysed pair-wise comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Bonferroni post hoc test on metabolites with statistically significant ANOVA results (p < 0.01).
outputs: Bonferroni post hoc test results (pairwise comparison p-values)

steps.description: p values lower than 0.05 were considered statistically significant.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter Bonferroni post hoc test results for statistical significance based on p < 0.05.
outputs: List of statistically significant pairwise comparisons for metabolites

steps.description: A principal component analysis was performed on log2 transformed and mean centred values for both metabolomics platforms with GeneMaths XT v.2.12.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on log2 transformed and mean centred metabolomics data.
inputs: Log2 transformed and mean centred metabolomics data

steps.description: The IBM SPSS statistic software package 20 (www.ibm.com) was used to perform the ANOVAs on the ratio of viable and germinated pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ANOVA on pollen viability and germination ratio data.

steps.description: followed by a Tukey's post hoc test.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Tukey's post hoc test following ANOVA on pollen data.
outputs: Tukey post hoc test results (pairwise comparison p-values)

steps.description: p values lower than 0.05 were considered statistically significant.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter Tukey post hoc test results for statistical significance based on p < 0.05.
outputs: List of statistically significant pairwise comparisons for pollen data

methods.description: Processing steps for raw LC-QTOF-MS data from untargeted metabolomics analysis, including data cleaning, feature extraction, and compound annotation using MetAlign and MSClust software.

steps.description: LC-QTOF-MS data were processed using MetAlign software (available from www.metalign.nl) to correct for the baseline and noise and to perform a mass spectral alignment of chromatograms as previously described by Tikunov et al. (2005) and De Vos et al. (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply MetAlign software for initial LC-QTOF-MS data processing (baseline/noise correction, alignment).

steps.description: MetAlign output was reduced by omitting mass data showing values lower than the detection threshold (20 ion counts) in more than two samples once the estimated peak signal was subtracted to the estimated noise signal.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter MetAlign output to remove mass data below a specified intensity threshold in multiple samples.

steps.description: Compound mass spectra and quantitative ions were extracted from the modified MetAlign outputs using a method described in Tikunov et al. (2012) by MSClust software (available from www.metalign.nl).
steps.searchable_summary: Use MSClust software to identify and extract compound features and quantitative ions from processed data.

steps.description: MSClust output was reduced by keeping compounds that were quantitatively present in all the replicates of one of the experimental treatments: heat stress or control.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter MSClust output to keep compounds present in all replicates of at least one treatment group.

steps.description: LC-QTOF-MS masses were kept for analysis when at least one sample had a relative abundance higher than 200 counts.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter mass data to keep masses with relative abundance above a threshold (200 counts) in at least one sample.

steps.description: If a quantitative ion automatically selected by MSClust showed saturation of the MS detector, this ion was replaced by its second or third isotopic ion.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and replace saturated quantitative ions with their second or third isotopic ions.
inputs: Filtered MSClust output with saturated ions
outputs: Filtered MSClust output with replaced ions

steps.description: Putative annotation of ions was performed with an in-house metabolite database and metabolite online databases Dictionary of Natural Products (http://dnp.chemnetbase.com/) and METLIN (http:// metlin.scripps.edu/).
steps.searchable_summary: Search internal and external databases to assign putative identities to extracted compounds/ions.
inputs: Processed and filtered mass/ion data (MSClust output)

steps.description: The annotation of compounds was performed according to the Metabolomics Standards Initiative requirements (Sumner et al. 2007). Identified compounds were annotated level I when NMR was performed on annotated compounds from the in-house library, level II when an analytical standard was used to annotate the compounds from the in-house library, or when a tandem mass spectrometry was performed and level III when compounds were annotated based on their mass.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign confidence levels (I, II, or III) to annotated compounds based on validation evidence.
inputs: Validation evidence (NMR, analytical standard, tandem MS, mass only)
outputs: Annotated compounds with assigned annotation levels
